repair_memories_applied.natural_language: When correctness fails due to output mismatch (e.g., small floating-point differences) in SigmoidCustom operator, do not implement 1/(1+exp(-x)) using Broadcast of 1.0 and Div; instead use AscendC::Reciprocal on the denominator to reduce precision loss.
When sigmoid output mismatch occurs due to numerical precision, do not implement sigmoid as 1/(1+exp(-x)) using Exp, Adds, and Reciprocal; instead use the tanh identity: y = 0.5 * (1 + tanh(x/2)).

repair_memories_applied.natural_language: When output mismatch occurs due to integer-to-float cast in kernel, do not directly cast totalSamples from uint32_t to float in the kernel; instead store totalSamplesFloat in tiling data and pass it as a separate parameter to the Init method.

When operator output mismatches reference due to manual sigmoid computation using separate tmp queues for Exp, Adds, Reciprocal, Mul, do not implement sigmoid manually; instead use AscendC::Sigmoid and consolidate temporary buffers into a single tmpQueue.

repair_memory_selection.selection_rationale: The current failure is correctness (output mismatch) for op ca6k_00286 (torch#1) after successful build and packaging.  Memories 3ba8664f (ca6k_00045 correctness due to sigmoid precision), 0ca36d0e and 48173ce0 (sigmoid correctness), and a85e42ea (swish manual sigmoid correctness) all address output mismatch caused by implementing sigmoid with Exp/Adds/Reciprocal and suggest using the tanh linear approximation or built-in Sigmoid; these are directly relevant if ca6k_00286 uses a similar manual sigmoid. Memory 77c4bf94 addresses output mismatch due to incorrect integer-to-float cast in the kernel, which could also be a cause of precision loss. Together they provide diverse hints about common correctness pitfalls in activation-type kernels.
